NCT04003155.name: SKYLIGHT-1
NCT04003155.group: Postmenopausal women with moderate-to-severe VMS, fezolinetant 45 mg PO QD vs placebo (wk 12)

allOutcomes.title: Change in moderate-to-severe VMS frequency per 24h at week 12 (primary, MD)

NCT04003155.snippet: Source: Lederman S et al. Lancet 2023;401:1091-1102 (SKYLIGHT-1).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 522 postmenopausal women aged 40-65 with moderate-to-severe vasomotor symptoms (minimum 7 VMS/day) randomized 1:1:1 double-blind to fezolinetant 30 mg PO daily (n=173), fezolinetant 45 mg PO daily (n=174), or placebo (n=175) for 12 weeks plus 40-week extension. Primary analysis compared fezolinetant 45 mg vs placebo.

evidence.label: Primary - VMS Frequency Change at Week 12
evidence.text: Change in moderate-to-severe VMS frequency per 24 hours at week 12: fezolinetant 45 mg -5.48 vs placebo -3.66; estimated treatment difference MD -1.82 (95% CI -2.58 to -1.07; P<0.001). Primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Secondary - VMS Severity and Sleep
evidence.text: Change in VMS severity score at week 12: fezolinetant 45 mg -0.39 vs placebo -0.21; ETD -0.18. Improvement in sleep (PROMIS Sleep Disturbance) and MENQoL vasomotor domain also significant.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR randomization stratified by smoking status and race. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo. D3 LOW - 93% completion at wk 12. D4 LOW - VMS captured via electronic diary (validated). D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: FEMALE; min age 40 Years; max age 65 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Subject has a body mass index ≥ 18 kg/m^2 and ≤ 38 kg/m^2.~* Subject must be seeking treatment or relief for vasomotor symptoms (VMS) associated with menopause and confirmed as menopausal per 1 of the following criteria at the screening visit:~  * Spontaneous amenorrhea for ≥ 12 consecutive months~ * Spontaneous amenorrhea for ≥ 6 months with biochemical criteria of menopause (follicle-stimulating hormone [FSH] > 40 IU/L); or~ * Having had bilateral oophorectomy ≥ 6 w...

NCT04003142.name: SKYLIGHT-2
NCT04003142.group: Postmenopausal women with moderate-to-severe VMS, fezolinetant 45 mg vs placebo (wk 12)

allOutcomes.title: Change in moderate-to-severe VMS frequency per 24h at week 12 (primary, MD)

NCT04003142.snippet: Source: Johnson KA et al. J Clin Endocrinol Metab 2023;108:1981-1997 (SKYLIGHT-2).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 500 postmenopausal women with moderate-to-severe VMS randomized 1:1:1 double-blind to fezolinetant 30 mg (n=166), fezolinetant 45 mg (n=167), or placebo (n=167) for 12 weeks plus 40-week extension. SKYLIGHT-2 was the replicate pivotal trial to SKYLIGHT-1.

evidence.label: Primary - VMS Frequency Change at Week 12
evidence.text: Change in moderate-to-severe VMS frequency per 24 hours at week 12: fezolinetant 45 mg -6.36 vs placebo -3.81; estimated treatment difference MD -2.55 (95% CI -3.37 to -1.73; P<0.001). Primary endpoint met, replicating SKYLIGHT-1.

evidence.label: Secondary - 40-Week Durability
evidence.text: Reduction in VMS frequency maintained through wk 52 (40-wk extension). Improvement in VMS severity (ETD -0.25), sleep, and MENQoL vasomotor/psychosocial domains all significant.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo. D3 LOW - 91% completion at wk 12. D4 LOW - electronic VMS diary. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: FEMALE; min age 40 Years; max age 65 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Subject has a body mass index ≥ 18 kg/m^2 and ≤ 38 kg/m^2.~* Subject must be seeking treatment or relief for vasomotor symptoms (VMS) associated with menopause and confirmed as menopausal per 1 of the following criteria at the screening visit:~  * Spontaneous amenorrhea for ≥ 12 consecutive months~ * Spontaneous amenorrhea for ≥ 6 months with biochemical criteria of menopause (follicle-stimulating hormone [FSH] > 40 IU/L); or~ * Having had bilateral oophorectomy ≥ 6 w...

title: RapidMeta Women Health | Fezolinetant for Menopausal Vasomotor Symptoms v1.1
